prompt: Using the layout of the provided fitness app dashboard, redesign it as a page from a 19th-century anatomical textbook, with detailed, hand-drawn medical illustrations of the human body representing the stats.
prompt_refined:
Generate an image transforming the layout and sectional arrangement of the fitness app dashboard from reference image 1 into a page from a 19th-century anatomical textbook. The output should feature an aged paper texture, classic scholarly serif typography, and detailed, hand-drawn medical illustrations in a monochrome or sepia palette.

Maintain the general vertical flow and prominent sections of image 1.
- Replace the mobile device's top status bar and the app's bottom navigation bar with a simple page number (e.g., 'Plate I') in the top right, consistent with a textbook.
- Reinterpret the 'Strava Suzie' profile area and 'Morning Run' title (top-left and central of image 1) as a scholarly heading or subject descriptor, like 'Anatomical Study: Human Physiology' or 'Observations on the Circulatory System.'
- The large map area (upper-middle section of image 1) must be replaced by a prominent, full-page-width, detailed hand-drawn anatomical illustration of the human body or a specific physiological system (e.g., skeletal, muscular, circulatory).
- For each of the six statistical data boxes (e.g., Distance, Avg Pace, Calories) in the lower-middle section, arranged in two columns in image 1, replace them with small, inset, detailed hand-drawn medical illustrations. Each illustration should conceptually represent its corresponding metric (e.g., a muscular leg for 'Distance', a heart diagram for 'Avg Heart Rate', a digestive system detail for 'Calories'). The original numerical values (e.g., '16.64 km', '159 bpm') must remain visible and legible, rendered in a classic 19th-century font adjacent to their respective illustrations.
- All other modern UI elements, such as the 'Congratulations' banner and 'View Analysis' button, should be omitted or replaced with static, informative text styled as textbook captions.
objects: Page number (e.g., 'Plate I') in the top right
Scholarly heading or subject descriptor (e.g., 'Anatomical Study: Human Physiology')
Prominent, full-page-width, detailed hand-drawn anatomical illustration
Six small, inset, detailed hand-drawn medical illustrations
Original numerical values (e.g., '16.64 km', '159 bpm') in classic 19th-century font
Static, informative text styled as textbook captions
Mobile device's top status bar (should not be present)
App's bottom navigation bar (should not be present)
